title: Editas Reports Promising Sickle Cell Treatment Results
text: Editas Medicine presented promising Phase 1/2/3 trial data for renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel), its experimental gene-edited therapy for severe sickle cell disease. Among 28 patients, results showed significant health improvements with nearly all patients remaining free from vaso-occlusive events, painful complications of sickle cell disease. Total hemoglobin increased from 9.8 g/dL at baseline to 13.8 g/dL at six months in 18 patients. Fetal hemoglobin levels rose rapidly and sustained above anti-sickling thresholds. Patients reported improvements in pain, physical function, and social activities. Treatment demonstrated tolerability consistent with standard stem cell transplant conditioning. Engraftment occurred in median timeframes of 23 days for neutrophils and 25 days for platelets, with only two serious adverse events possibly related to treatment. Market capitalization $190 million with strong liquidity position and current ratio of 3.75. Cash reserves of $322 million supporting operations into Q2 2026. Recent revenue growth of 151% over twelve months. Multiple analyst upgrades followed announcement with BofA Securities and Evercore ISI initiating Buy ratings.
